---
title: "My first real blog"
date: "2026-05-27"
image: "matplotlib_bar.png"
---

the palmerpenguins dataset is **always** useful


```{python}
from palmerpenguins import load_penguins
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

penguins_df = load_penguins()
penguins_cleaned_df = penguins_df.dropna()

species_counts = penguins_cleaned_df['species'].value_counts()
```

# Showing Matplotlib Charts

We can display matplotlib charts directly:

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plot = plt.figure()
plot.set_figwidth(6)
plot.set_figheight(3)

plt.bar(species_counts.index, species_counts.values)
```


```{python}
plot.savefig("matplotlib_bar.png")
```
